data.description: Emerge is a browser-based interactive codebase and dependency visualization tool for many different programming languages. It supports some basic code quality and graph metrics and provides a simple and intuitive way to explore and analyze a codebase by using graph structures. - glato/emerge

alt=\"PyPI\" data-canonical-src=\"https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/emerge-viz\" style=\"max-width: 100%;\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eEmerge\u003c/strong\u003e (or \u003cstrong\u003eemerge-viz\u003c/strong\u003e) is an interactive code analysis tool to gather insights about source code structure, metrics, dependencies and complexity of software projects. You can scan the source code of a project, calculate metric results and statistics, generate an interactive web app with graph structures (e.g. a dependency graph or a filesystem graph) and export the results in some file formats. Emerge currently has parsing support for the following languages: \u003ccode\u003eC\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eC++\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eGroovy\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eJava\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eJavaScript\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eTypeScript\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eKotlin\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eObjC\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eRuby\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eSwift\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003ePython\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eGo\u003c/code\u003e. The structure, coloring and clustering is calculated and based on the idea of combining a \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/d3/d3-force\"\u003eforce-directed graph\u003c/a\u003e simulation and \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/taynaud/python-louvain\"\u003eLouvain modularity\u003c/a\u003e. emerge is mainly written in Python 3 and is tested on macOS, linux and modern web browsers (i.e. latest dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eto appear by coming out of something or out from behind something\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eto become known, especially as a result of examining something or asking questions about it\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e \u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe main goal of this project is to create a free/ open source tool, that can easily be used by anyone with interest in software development, architecture, metrics and visualization to gather more insights about those topics. It should facilitate/ support getting a better understanding of a given software project by using an \u003ca href=\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Exploratory_data_analysis\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eexploratory approach\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e \u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" \u003ccode\u003eJavaScript\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eTypeScript\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eKotlin\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eObjC\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eRuby\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eSwift\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003ePython\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eBasic entity scan/extraction (e.g. classes) for the following languages: \u003ccode\u003eGroovy\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eJava\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eKotlin\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eSwift\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eImplementation of the following software metrics: SLOC, Whitespace Complexity (impl. by A. Tornhill), Number of Methods, Fan-In/Fan-Out, Modularity (Louvain)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eExperimental implementation of additional \u003ccode\u003egit-based\u003c/code\u003e metrics (SLOC, Whitespace Complexity, Change Coupling)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eInfer meaning by feature/semantic keyword extraction based on \u003ca href=\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tf%E2%80%93idf\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eterm frequency-inverse document frequency\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eLogging support with configurable log levels\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eConfiguration support based on YAML syntax to configure multiple/specific analyses\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eCreate a language/project configuration directly from an included configuration template\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eExport of scan results/ metrics/ statistics for the following formats/ outputs\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eFile scan\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eDependency graph\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eEntity scan\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eDependency graph\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eInheritance graph\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eComplete graph (composition of dependency and inheritance graph)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eIncludes the extraction of \u003ccode\u003eSwiftUI\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003eComposable\u003c/code\u003e declarative UI entities\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eA Filesystem graph that shows the project filesystem hierarchy as a graph\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"http://graphml.graphdrawing.org\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eGraphML\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eJavaScript format suited for a \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/d3/d3-force\"\u003eD3 force graph simulation\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eInteractive HTML/ web application for interactive, exploratory analysis and data visualization of your project based on graph structures\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eHTML app is based on \u003ca href=\"https://getbootstrap.com\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eBootstrap\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eForce-directed graph simulation by \u003ca href=\"https://d3js.org\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eD3\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eThe node colors are based on \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/taynaud/python-louvain\"\u003eLouvain modularity\u003c/a\u003e with a bit of post-processing to make the graph coloring more deterministic and stable\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eFast full-screen UI rendering on HTML canvas\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eVisualization of files, entities and given metrics\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://github.com/coliff/dark-mode-switch\"\u003eDark mode support\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eVisual live search (OR'ed with multiple search terms) of entities\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eThe option to include a semantic search based on \u003ca href=\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tf%E2%80%93idf\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eterm frequency-inverse document frequency\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eThe option to include git-based metainformation e.g. contributor names\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eSelection and highlighting of individual nodes\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://github.com/AndriiHeonia/hull\"\u003eConcave hull\u003c/a\u003e visualization of single clusters\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://github.com/mourner/simpleheat\"\u003eHeatmap\u003c/a\u003e visualization support of potentially harmful nodes based on a SLOC/Fan-Out score\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e[Heatmap] visualization of of \u003ccode\u003egit-based\u003c/code\u003e metrics e.g. code churn\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eDisplay of cluster metrics to facilitate comparability\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eInteractivity given by translation, zooming, dragging and hovering over nodes\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eTabular console output\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eTabular file output\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eJSON file output\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e \u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" The only prerequisite is to have a Docker engine. For example the \u003ca href=\"https://docs.docker.com/engine/install/\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eDocker Desktop\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003ePrepare your working folder like this\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"config.yml\n📁export\n📁source\"\u003e\u003cpre lang=\"text\" class=\"notranslate\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003econfig.yml\n📁export\n📁source\n\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003econfig.yml = configuration file\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eexport = folder for your export\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003esource = folder which contains your source code.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe command to run the analysis is than:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"docker run --rm -v \u0026lt;YOUR_WORKING_FOLDER_PATH\u0026gt;:/tmp/emerge achtelik/emerge:2.0.0 /tmp/emerge/config.yml\"\u003e\u003cpre lang=\"text\" class=\"notranslate\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003edocker run --rm -v \u0026lt;YOUR_WORKING_FOLDER_PATH\u0026gt;:/tmp/emerge achtelik/emerge:2.0.0 /tmp/emerge/config.yml\n\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe last parameter is the path to the config.yml inside the Docker container.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e⚡You can ignore the Pyperclip error at the end of the run.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf you use the suggestion from above, than pay attention that your \u003ccode\u003eanalyses.source_directory\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003eexport.directory\u003c/code\u003e path have to start with \u003ccode\u003e/tmp/emerge\u003c/code\u003e. This is necessary because your analysis is running inside the Docker container.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFor example:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"---\nproject_name: java_project_example\nloglevel: info\nanalyses:\n- analysis_name: full java check\n  source_directory: /tmp/emerge/source\n  .\n  .\n  .\n  export:\n  - directory: /tmp/emerge/export\n  .\n  .\n  .\"\u003e\u003cpre lang=\"text\" class=\"notranslate\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e---\nproject_name: java_project_example\nloglevel: info\nanalyses:\n- analysis_name: full java check\n  source_directory: /tmp/emerge/source\n  .\n  .\n  .\n  export:\n  - directory: /tmp/emerge/export\n  .\n  .\n  .\n\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e💡The Docker container itself is path independent. Feel free to use your own volume mount and project config paths.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eHow to install and use emerge as a user\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-how-to-install-and-use-emerge-as-a-user\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: How to install and use emerge as a user\" href=\"#how-to-install-and-use-emerge-as-a-user\"\u003e\u003csvg class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eBasically there are two ways to install emerge. If you're familiar with \u003ccode\u003epip\u003c/code\u003e (a virtual environment by using \u003ccode\u003epyenv\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003evirtualenv\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003evirtualenvwrapper\u003c/code\u003e is recommended, but not needed) you can simply install the latest version of emerge with the following few steps.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv  - dependency_graph\n  - source_lines_of_code\n  - number_of_methods\n  - fan_in_out\n  - louvain_modularity\n  - tfidf\n  export:\n  - directory: /Users/user1/emerge/project/export\n  - graphml\n  - json\n  - tabular_file\n  - tabular_console_overall\n  - d3\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003e---\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eproject_name\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003esource_lines_of_code\u003c/span\u003e\n  - \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003enumber_of_methods\u003c/span\u003e\n  - \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003efan_in_out\u003c/span\u003e\n  - \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003elouvain_modularity\u003c/span\u003e\n  - \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003etfidf\u003c/span\u003e\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003etabular_console_overall\u003c/span\u003e\n  - \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003ed3\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eSometimes it can make description\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eanalysis_name\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003ea specific analysis name\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003esource_directory\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003ethe source directory where the recursive file scan should start\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003egit_directory\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003ethe git repo directory, if git metrics should be included\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003egit_commit_limit\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003ehow many commits from the last commit should be mined? default: \u003ccode\u003e150\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003egit_exclude_merge_commits\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eshould merge commits be excluded from mining all metrics? default: \u003ccode\u003etrue\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eignore_files_containing\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eexclude file names from the scan that contain the given substrings\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eignore_directories_containing\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eexclude directory names from the scan that contain the given substrings\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eonly_permit_languages\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003epossible values include: java, kotlin, objc, swift, ruby, groovy, javascript, c - explicitly prevents any other language from scanning besides the one you set here\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eonly_permit_file_extensions\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eexplicitly permit the following file extensions you set here, e.g. \u003ccode\u003e.java\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eonly_permit_files_matching_absolute_path\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eonly the following list of absolute file paths is permitted for the file scan, e.g. \u003ccode\u003e[/Users/user1/source/file1.java]\u003c/code\u003e. The files should follow \u003ccode\u003esource_directory\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eignore_dependencies_containing\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eignore every dependency included in this list of substrings, e.g. \u003ccode\u003ejava.util\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eignore_dependencies_matching\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eignore every dependency matching any of the regular expressions in this list of substrings, e.g. \u003ccode\u003e^java\\.util\\.\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eignore_entities_containing\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eignore every entity included in this list of substrings, e.g. \u003ccode\u003eNotRelevantClass\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eignore_entities_matching\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eignore every entity matching any of the regular expressions in this list of substrings, e.g. \u003ccode\u003e^Test\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eimport_aliases\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003edefine a list of import aliases, i.e. replace substrings within a full dependency path, e.g. \u003ccode\u003e\"@foo\": src/foo\u003c/code\u003e will replace any \u003ccode\u003e@foo\u003c/code\u003e alias by \u003ccode\u003esrc/foo\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eoverride_resolve_dependencies\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eif supported by the language parser, force every dependency in this list to be resolved\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eoverride_do_not_resolve_dependencies\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eif supported by the language parser, force every dependency in this list NOT to be resolved (i.e. treated as a global dependency)\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003efile_scan\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eperform a file scan, contains the metrics that should be applied on every source file\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eentity_scan\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eperform an entity scan, contains the metrics that should be applied on every entity (e.g. on every class)\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eexport\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003econtains any export formats that should be created as output\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eappconfig\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003econtains any configurable app config parameters\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cdiv and project dependent. The following examples should help to recognize certain patterns through indicators and hints.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e⭐️ Modularity\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-️-modularity\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: ⭐️ Modularity\" href=\"#️-modularity\"\u003e\u003csvg class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe magic of uncovering modularity lies in applying a community detection algorithm e.g. Louvain optimization to a force-directed graph, so that both distances and coloring influence the result. The following example includes several indicators for a modular codebase.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIn the first example on the left you can spot multiple coherent colored clusters which show a low coupling by a certain distance (= generated by the force-directed graph).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIn the second example on the right the same graph is rendered with activated cluster hulls. On this example the hulls show minimal to no overlapping. Such hints can be indicators for a good software architecture e.g. in terms of \u003cem\u003emodularity\u003c/em\u003e, \u003cem\u003eabstraction\u003c/em\u003e and well defined \u003cem\u003einterfaces\u003c/em\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cp align=\"center\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003ca target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer nofollow\" modularity e.g. cluster 1 and 2.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eAt the same time you can see another cluster 3 that shows increased overlappings with other clusters. In addition to that an active SLOC metric even shows some huge entities (e.g. classes) which could indicate code smells like \u003cem\u003egod classes\u003c/em\u003e. Such code smells like increased coupling and god classes may indicate increased maintenace efforts and error-proneness.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cp align=\"center\" Foote, J. Yoder, 1997). This kind of graph often represents a less optimal architecture. To verify this kind of \u003cem\u003espaghetti code jungle\u003c/em\u003e, one can simply enable hull rendering for all clusters to finally determine: there is only one big cluster after all.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp align=\"center\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003ca target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer nofollow\" 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eSometimes it can help to better understand the complexity of a software architecture if irrelevant dependencies are ignored.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eBesides being shocked to see the Big Ball of Mud with irrelevant dependencies like java.lang, java.util or any third party dependencies that does not directly belong to a project ...\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e... you can remove irrelevant dependencies configuratively with the key \u003ccode\u003eignore_dependencies_containing\u003c/code\u003e (or \u003ccode\u003eignore_dependencies_matching\u003c/code\u003e if you prefer regular expressions). With a comparatively activated fan-out metric, one recognizes more scattering, some distant hub nodes and clearer clusters. All of these are possible clues to the real (= often more understandable) architecture underneath.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cp align=\"center\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003ca <p dir="auto"><strong>Emerge</strong> (or <strong>emerge-viz</strong>) is an interactive code analysis tool to gather insights about source code structure, metrics, dependencies and complexity of software projects. You can scan the source code of a project, calculate metric results and statistics, generate an interactive web app with graph structures (e.g. a dependency graph or a filesystem graph) and export the results in some file formats. Emerge currently has parsing support for the following languages: <code>C</code>, <code>C++</code>, <code>Groovy</code>, <code>Java</code>, <code>JavaScript</code>, <code>TypeScript</code>, <code>Kotlin</code>, <code>ObjC</code>, <code>Ruby</code>, <code>Swift</code>, <code>Python</code>, <code>Go</code>. The structure, coloring and clustering is calculated and based on the idea of combining a <a href="https://github.com/d3/d3-force">force-directed graph</a> simulation and <a href="https://github.com/taynaud/python-louvain">Louvain modularity</a>. emerge is mainly written in Python 3 and is tested on macOS, linux and modern web browsers (i.e. latest Safari, Chrome, Firefox, Edge).</p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Goals of this project</h2><a id="user-content-goals-of-this-project" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Goals of this project" href="#goals-of-this-project"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><em>emerge</em> (/ɪˈməːdʒ/)</p>
<blockquote>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>to appear by coming out of something or out from behind something</li>
<li>to become known, especially as a result of examining something or asking questions about it</li>
</ul>
</blockquote>
<p dir="auto"> </p>
<p dir="auto">The main goal of this project is to create a free/ open source tool, that can easily be used by anyone with interest in software development, architecture, metrics and visualization to gather more insights about those topics. It should facilitate/ support getting a better understanding of a given software project by using an <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Exploratory_data_analysis" rel="nofollow">exploratory approach</a>.</p>
<p dir="auto"> </p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">The following features are currently supported by emerge</h2><a id="user-content-the-following-features-are-currently-supported-by-emerge" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: The following features are currently supported by emerge" href="#the-following-features-are-currently-supported-by-emerge"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>File scan support for the following languages: <code>C</code>, <code>C++</code>, <code>Groovy</code>, <code>Java</code>, <code>JavaScript</code>, <code>TypeScript</code>, <code>Kotlin</code>, <code>ObjC</code>, <code>Ruby</code>, <code>Swift</code>, <code>Python</code></li>
<li>Basic entity scan/extraction (e.g. classes) for the following languages: <code>Groovy</code>, <code>Java</code>, <code>Kotlin</code>, <code>Swift</code></li>
<li>Implementation of the following software metrics: SLOC, Whitespace Complexity (impl. by A. Tornhill), Number of Methods, Fan-In/Fan-Out, Modularity (Louvain)</li>
<li>Experimental implementation of additional <code>git-based</code> metrics (SLOC, Whitespace Complexity, Change Coupling)</li>
<li>Infer meaning by feature/semantic keyword extraction based on <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tf%E2%80%93idf" rel="nofollow">term frequency-inverse document frequency</a></li>
<li>Logging support with configurable log levels</li>
<li>Configuration support based on YAML syntax to configure multiple/specific analyses</li>
<li>Create a language/project configuration directly from an included configuration template</li>
<li>Export of scan results/ metrics/ statistics for the following formats/ outputs
<ul dir="auto">
<li>File scan
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Dependency graph</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>Entity scan
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Dependency graph</li>
<li>Inheritance graph</li>
<li>Complete graph (composition of dependency and inheritance graph)</li>
<li>Includes the extraction of <code>SwiftUI</code> and <code>Composable</code> declarative UI entities</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>A Filesystem graph that shows the project filesystem hierarchy as a graph</li>
<li><a href="http://graphml.graphdrawing.org" rel="nofollow">GraphML</a></li>
<li>JavaScript format suited for a <a href="https://github.com/d3/d3-force">D3 force graph simulation</a></li>
<li>Interactive HTML/ web application for interactive, exploratory analysis and data visualization of your project based on graph structures
<ul dir="auto">
<li>HTML app is based on <a href="https://getbootstrap.com" rel="nofollow">Bootstrap</a></li>
<li>Force-directed graph simulation by <a href="https://d3js.org" rel="nofollow">D3</a></li>
<li>The node colors are based on <a href="https://github.com/taynaud/python-louvain">Louvain modularity</a> with a bit of post-processing to make the graph coloring more deterministic and stable</li>
<li>Fast full-screen UI rendering on HTML canvas</li>
<li>Visualization of files, entities and given metrics</li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/coliff/dark-mode-switch">Dark mode support</a></li>
<li>Visual live search (OR'ed with multiple search terms) of entities</li>
<li>The option to include a semantic search based on <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tf%E2%80%93idf" rel="nofollow">term frequency-inverse document frequency</a></li>
<li>The option to include git-based metainformation e.g. contributor names</li>
<li>Selection and highlighting of individual nodes</li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/AndriiHeonia/hull">Concave hull</a> visualization of single clusters</li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/mourner/simpleheat">Heatmap</a> visualization support of potentially harmful nodes based on a SLOC/Fan-Out score</li>
<li>[Heatmap] visualization of of <code>git-based</code> metrics e.g. code churn</li>
<li>Display of cluster metrics to facilitate comparability</li>
<li>Interactivity given by translation, zooming, dragging and hovering over nodes</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>Tabular console output</li>
<li>Tabular file output</li>
<li>JSON file output</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto"> </p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">How to use emerge as Docker container</h2><a id="user-content-how-to-use-emerge-as-docker-container" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: How to use emerge as Docker container" href="#how-to-use-emerge-as-docker-container"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The easiest way to use emerge in a pre build Docker container. The only prerequisite is to have a Docker engine. For example the <a href="https://docs.docker.com/engine/install/" rel="nofollow">Docker Desktop</a>.</p>
<p dir="auto">Prepare your working folder like this</p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre lang="text" class="notranslate"><code>config.yml
📁export
📁source
</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

📁export
📁source" tabindex="0" role="button">

<li>config.yml = configuration file</li>
<li>export = folder for your export</li>
<li>source = folder which contains your source code.</li>

<p dir="auto">The command to run the analysis is than:</p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre lang="text" class="notranslate"><code>docker run --rm -v <YOUR_WORKING_FOLDER_PATH>:/tmp/emerge achtelik/emerge:2.0.0 /tmp/emerge/config.yml
</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<p dir="auto">The last parameter is the path to the config.yml inside the Docker container.</p>
<p dir="auto">⚡You can ignore the Pyperclip error at the end of the run.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">config.yml</h3><a id="user-content-configyml" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: config.yml" href="#configyml"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">If you use the suggestion from above, than pay attention that your <code>analyses.source_directory</code> and <code>export.directory</code> path have to start with <code>/tmp/emerge</code>. This is necessary because your analysis is running inside the Docker container.</p>
<p dir="auto">For example:</p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre lang="text" class="notranslate"><code>---
project_name: java_project_example
loglevel: info
analyses:
- analysis_name: full java check
  source_directory: /tmp/emerge/source
  .
  .
  .
  export:
  - directory: /tmp/emerge/export
  .
  .
  .
</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="---
project_name: java_project_example
loglevel: info
analyses:
- analysis_name: full java check
  source_directory: /tmp/emerge/source
  .
  .
  .
  export:
  - directory: /tmp/emerge/export
  .
  .
  ." tabindex="0" role="button">
      <svg aria-hidden="true" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon">

<p dir="auto">💡The Docker container itself is path independent. Feel free to use your own volume mount and project config paths.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">How to install and use emerge as a user</h2><a id="user-content-how-to-install-and-use-emerge-as-a-user" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: How to install and use emerge as a user" href="#how-to-install-and-use-emerge-as-a-user"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Basically there are two ways to install emerge. If you're familiar with <code>pip</code> (a virtual environment by using <code>pyenv</code>, <code>virtualenv</code> and <code>virtualenvwrapper</code> is recommended, but not needed) you can simply install the latest version of emerge with the following few steps.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">0️⃣ ~ (Optional) Setup a virtual environment with pyenv</h3><a id="user-content-0️⃣--optional-setup-a-virtual-environment-with-pyenv" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 0️⃣ ~ (Optional) Setup a virtual environment with pyenv" href="#0️⃣--optional-setup-a-virtual-environment-with-pyenv"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The recommended way would be to use a virtual env, you can do this by using the following example:</p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre lang="text" class="notranslate"><code>pyenv install 3.10.0
pyenv virtualenv 3.10.0 venv-3.10.0
pyenv activate venv-3.10.0
</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="pyenv install 3.10.0
pyenv virtualenv 3.10.0 venv-3.10.0
pyenv activate venv-3.10.0" tabindex="0" role="button">

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">1️⃣ ~ Install emerge with pip</h3><a id="user-content-1️⃣--install-emerge-with-pip" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 1️⃣ ~ Install emerge with pip" href="#1️⃣--install-emerge-with-pip"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">You can simply install emerge by using <code>pip</code>.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">1️⃣.1️⃣ ~ (<em>ubuntu</em>) install prerequisites</h3><a id="user-content-1️⃣1️⃣--ubuntu-install-prerequisites" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 1️⃣.1️⃣ ~ (ubuntu) install prerequisites" href="#1️⃣1️⃣--ubuntu-install-prerequisites"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">On Ubuntu 20.04+ pleaase make sure that the packages <code>graphviz</code> and <code>graphviz-dev</code> are installed, i.e.</p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre lang="text" class="notranslate"><code>apt-get install graphviz graphviz-dev
</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">1️⃣.2️⃣ ~ Install with pip</h3><a id="user-content-1️⃣2️⃣--install-with-pip" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 1️⃣.2️⃣ ~ Install with pip" href="#1️⃣2️⃣--install-with-pip"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Either install as new package with:</p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre lang="text" class="notranslate"><code>pip install emerge-viz
</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<p dir="auto">and then simply execute it like this:</p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre lang="text" class="notranslate"><code>(emerge) user@host ~ % emerge

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  -c YAMLCONFIG, --config YAMLCONFIG
                        set yaml config file
  -v, --verbose         set logging level to INFO
  -d, --debug           set logging level to DEBUG
  -e, --error           set logging level to ERROR

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">2️⃣ ~ Create and adjust project configuration</h3><a id="user-content-2️⃣--create-and-adjust-project-configuration" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 2️⃣ ~ Create and adjust project configuration" href="#2️⃣--create-and-adjust-project-configuration"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">You can create a simple project config adhoc from the command line and then simply adjust the necessary source/export paths</p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre lang="text" class="notranslate"><code>(emerge) user@host tmp % pwd
/Users/user1/tmp
(emerge) user@host tmp % emerge -a java
✅ created config file from template: /Users/user1/tmp/java-template.yaml
</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

/Users/user1/tmp
(emerge) user@host tmp % emerge -a java
✅ created config file from template: /Users/user1/tmp/java-template.yaml" tabindex="0" role="button">

<p dir="auto">and then simply adjust the necessary paths (<code>analyses/source_directory</code> and <code>export/directory</code>):</p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre lang="text" class="notranslate"><code>(emerge) user@host tmp % cat java-template.yaml
---
project_name: java_project_example
loglevel: info
analyses:
- analysis_name: full java check
  source_directory: /Users/user1/emerge/project/source
  only_permit_languages:
  - java
  only_permit_file_extensions:
  - .java
  file_scan:
  - number_of_methods
  - source_lines_of_code
  - dependency_graph
  - fan_in_out
  - louvain_modularity
  - tfidf
  entity_scan:
  - dependency_graph
  - source_lines_of_code
  - number_of_methods
  - fan_in_out
  - louvain_modularity
  - tfidf
  export:
  - directory: /Users/user1/emerge/project/export
  - graphml
  - json
  - tabular_file
  - tabular_console_overall
  - d3
(emerge) user@host tmp %
</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">How to install and use emerge from source (e.g. for development)</h2><a id="user-content-how-to-install-and-use-emerge-from-source-eg-for-development" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: How to install and use emerge from source (e.g. for development)" href="#how-to-install-and-use-emerge-from-source-eg-for-development"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>

<p dir="auto">If you encounter the following error on an Apple silicon Mac</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>pygraphviz/graphviz_wrap.c:2711:10: fatal error: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">'</span>graphviz/cgraph.h<span class="pl-pds">'</span></span> file not found
      <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span>include "graphviz/cgraph.h"</span>
               ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      1 error generated.</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<p dir="auto">you need to run the following command once to update the pygraphviz include directories for the new homebrew environment</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>pip install --global-option=build_ext --global-option=<span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>-I<span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">$(</span>brew --prefix graphviz<span class="pl-pds">)</span></span>/include/<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span> --global-option=<span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>-L<span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">$(</span>brew --prefix graphviz<span class="pl-pds">)</span></span>/lib/<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span> pygraphviz</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<p dir="auto">Check of you have the latest Python 3 installed on your macOS. I recommend installing/using Python 3 from <a href="https://brew.sh" rel="nofollow">Homebrew</a>. Create a Python 3 virtual environment (optionally within the project structure)</p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre lang="text" class="notranslate"><code>cd emerge
pip3 install virtualenv
virtualenv -p python3 venv
</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<p dir="auto">Now just copy the above mentioned <code>file://</code> path to any modern web browser and interactively expore the emerge codebase 😉</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">8️⃣.1️⃣ ~ Currently emerge offers the following keyboard shortcuts in the interactive web app</h4><a id="user-content-8️⃣1️⃣--currently-emerge-offers-the-following-keyboard-shortcuts-in-the-interactive-web-app" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 8️⃣.1️⃣ ~ Currently emerge offers the following keyboard shortcuts in the interactive web app" href="#8️⃣1️⃣--currently-emerge-offers-the-following-keyboard-shortcuts-in-the-interactive-web-app"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>ℹ️ Hovering over a node and pressing ⬆️ + <code>s</code> to select and highlight or deselect a specific node</li>
<li>ℹ️ Resetting the currently active node selection by pressing ⬆️ +  <code>r</code></li>
<li>ℹ️ Fading all unselected nodes to have a more highlighted visualization of you currenty selected nodes by pressing ⬆️ + <code>f</code></li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">And now let's make this more interesting ...</p>
<p dir="auto"> </p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Further configuration (using emerge on other projects)</h2><a id="user-content-further-configuration-using-emerge-on-other-projects" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Further configuration (using emerge on other projects)" href="#further-configuration-using-emerge-on-other-projects"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">If you wand to use emerge on other projects, you can simple copy or customize one of the existing configuration templates from the <code>emerge/configs</code> directory.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">9️⃣ ~ Scan a real project</h3><a id="user-content-9️⃣--scan-a-real-project" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 9️⃣ ~ Scan a real project" href="#9️⃣--scan-a-real-project"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">For a quick run, it should be enough to adjust <code>source_directory</code>, <code>directory</code> in <code>export</code>.</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-yaml notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>---
<span class="pl-ent">project_name</span>: <span class="pl-s">c-example-project</span>
<span class="pl-ent">loglevel</span>: <span class="pl-s">info</span>
<span class="pl-ent">analyses</span>:
- <span class="pl-ent">analysis_name</span>: <span class="pl-s">check_c_files</span>
  <span class="pl-ent">source_directory</span>: <span class="pl-s">/Users/user1/emerge/project/source/github/linux-5.8.5/crypto</span>
  <span class="pl-ent">only_permit_languages</span>:
  - <span class="pl-s">c</span>
  <span class="pl-ent">only_permit_file_extensions</span>:
  - <span class="pl-s">.c</span>
  - <span class="pl-s">.h</span>
  <span class="pl-ent">ignore_dependencies_containing</span>:
  - <span class="pl-s">string.h</span>
  <span class="pl-ent">ignore_dependencies_matching</span>:
  - <span class="pl-s">^test_(.*)\.h$</span>
  <span class="pl-ent">file_scan</span>:
  - <span class="pl-s">number_of_methods</span>
  - <span class="pl-s">source_lines_of_code</span>
  - <span class="pl-s">dependency_graph</span>
  - <span class="pl-s">louvain_modularity</span>
  - <span class="pl-s">fan_in_out</span>
  - <span class="pl-s">tfidf</span>
  <span class="pl-ent">export</span>:
  - <span class="pl-ent">directory</span>: <span class="pl-s">/Users/user1/emerge/project/export</span>
  - <span class="pl-s">graphml</span>
  - <span class="pl-s">json</span>
  - <span class="pl-s">tabular_file</span>
  - <span class="pl-s">tabular_console_overall</span>
  - <span class="pl-c1">d3</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="---

analyses:
- analysis_name: check_c_files

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">1️⃣0️⃣ ~ Run emerge with a specific yaml configuration</h3><a id="user-content-1️⃣0️⃣--run-emerge-with-a-specific-yaml-configuration" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 1️⃣0️⃣ ~ Run emerge with a specific yaml configuration" href="#1️⃣0️⃣--run-emerge-with-a-specific-yaml-configuration"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">After customizing a present config (e.g. <code>config/c-template.yaml</code>) or creating your own, just run emerge again with this new config</p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre lang="text" class="notranslate"><code>python emerge.py -c configs/c-template.yaml
</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<p dir="auto">After the scan, your scan output (including your interactive web app) can be found at the directory that you created and set in the config parameter <code>export</code> -> <code>directory</code>, as seen in the logs above.</p>
<p dir="auto">A full YAML configuration that contains both file and entity scan has the following format:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-yaml notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>---
<span class="pl-ent">project_name</span>: <span class="pl-s">java_project_example</span>
<span class="pl-ent">loglevel</span>: <span class="pl-s">info</span>
<span class="pl-ent">analyses</span>:
- <span class="pl-ent">analysis_name</span>: <span class="pl-s">check_java_files_and_classes</span>
  <span class="pl-ent">source_directory</span>: <span class="pl-s">/Users/user1/emerge/project/source</span>
  <span class="pl-ent">only_permit_languages</span>:
  - <span class="pl-s">java</span>
  <span class="pl-ent">only_permit_file_extensions</span>:
  - <span class="pl-s">.java</span>
  <span class="pl-ent">ignore_dependencies_containing</span>:
  - <span class="pl-s">java.util</span>
  <span class="pl-ent">file_scan</span>:
  - <span class="pl-s">number_of_methods</span>
  - <span class="pl-s">source_lines_of_code</span>
  - <span class="pl-s">dependency_graph</span>
  - <span class="pl-s">fan_in_out</span>
  - <span class="pl-s">louvain_modularity</span>
  - <span class="pl-s">tfidf</span>
  <span class="pl-ent">entity_scan</span>:
  - <span class="pl-s">dependency_graph</span>
  - <span class="pl-s">source_lines_of_code</span>
  - <span class="pl-s">number_of_methods</span>
  - <span class="pl-s">fan_in_out</span>
  - <span class="pl-s">louvain_modularity</span>
  - <span class="pl-s">tfidf</span>
  <span class="pl-ent">export</span>:
  - <span class="pl-ent">directory</span>: <span class="pl-s">/Users/user1/emerge/project/export</span>
  - <span class="pl-s">graphml</span>
  - <span class="pl-s">json</span>
  - <span class="pl-s">tabular_file</span>
  - <span class="pl-s">tabular_console_overall</span>
  - <span class="pl-c1">d3</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="---
project_name: java_project_example
loglevel: info
analyses:
- analysis_name: check_java_files_and_classes
  source_directory: /Users/user1/emerge/project/source
  only_permit_languages:
  - java
  only_permit_file_extensions:
  - .java
  ignore_dependencies_containing:
  - java.util
  file_scan:
  - number_of_methods
  - source_lines_of_code
  - dependency_graph
  - fan_in_out
  - louvain_modularity
  - tfidf
  entity_scan:
  - dependency_graph
  - source_lines_of_code
  - number_of_methods
  - fan_in_out
  - louvain_modularity
  - tfidf
  export:
  - directory: /Users/user1/emerge/project/export
  - graphml
  - json
  - tabular_file
  - tabular_console_overall
  - d3" tabindex="0" role="button">
      <svg aria-hidden="true" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon">

<p dir="auto">Sometimes it can make sense to exclude platform-usual dependencies or dependencies which do not contribute much to the understanding of a project. A good starting point for e.g. an <strong>Android project</strong> could the following <code>ignore_dependencies_containing</code> section:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-yaml notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-ent">ignore_dependencies_containing</span>:
  - <span class="pl-s">android</span>
  - <span class="pl-s">java</span>
  - <span class="pl-s">javax</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="ignore_dependencies_containing:
  - android
  - java
  - javax" tabindex="0" role="button">
      <svg aria-hidden="true" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon">
    <path d="M0 6.75C0 5.784.784 5 1.75 5h1.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-1.5a.25.25 0 0 0-.25.25v7.5c0 .138.112.25.25.25h7.5a.25.25 0 0 0 .25-.25v-1.5a.75.75 0 0 1 1.5 0v1.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 9.25 16h-7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 0 14.25Z"></path><path d="M5 1.75C5 .784 5.784 0 6.75 0h7.5C15.216 0 16 .784 16 1.75v7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 14.25 11h-7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 5 9.25Zm1.75-.25a.25.25 0 0 0-.25.25v7.5c0 .138.112.25.25.25h7.5a.25.25 0 0 0 .25-.25v-7.5a.25.25 0 0 0-.25-.25Z"></path>
</svg>
      <svg aria-hidden="true" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-check js-clipboard-check-icon color-fg-success d-none">
    <path d="M13.78 4.22a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.06l-7.25 7.25a.75.75 0 0 1-1.06 0L2.22 9.28a.751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018L6 10.94l6.72-6.72a.75.75 0 0 1 1.06 0Z"></path>

<p dir="auto">or for an <strong>iOS project</strong> the following <code>ignore_entities_containing</code> section often makes sense e.g. to not consider SwiftUI previews for the graph output:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-yaml notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-ent">ignore_entities_containing</span>:
  - <span class="pl-s">_Previews</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="ignore_entities_containing:
  - _Previews" tabindex="0" role="button">

<p dir="auto">The yaml configuration is basically defined at the following levels:</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">project level</h2><a id="user-content-project-level" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: project level" href="#project-level"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>key</th>
<th>value/ description</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>project_name</code></td>
<td>a project name for all analyses, scans and exports</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>loglevel</code></td>
<td>set a loglevel: <code>error</code> (silent, only errors), <code>info</code> (includes <code>error</code>) gives you basic logs about control flow, <code>debug</code> (includes <code>info</code>) will produce a lot of debug logs</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>analyses</code></td>
<td>an array of analyses that can be configured individually, thus a project can contain one to many analyses.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">analysis level</h2><a id="user-content-analysis-level" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: analysis level" href="#analysis-level"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>key</th>
<th>value/ description</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>analysis_name</code></td>
<td>a specific analysis name</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>source_directory</code></td>
<td>the source directory where the recursive file scan should start</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>git_directory</code></td>
<td>the git repo directory, if git metrics should be included</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>git_commit_limit</code></td>
<td>how many commits from the last commit should be mined? default: <code>150</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>git_exclude_merge_commits</code></td>
<td>should merge commits be excluded from mining all metrics? default: <code>true</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>ignore_files_containing</code></td>
<td>exclude file names from the scan that contain the given substrings</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>ignore_directories_containing</code></td>
<td>exclude directory names from the scan that contain the given substrings</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>only_permit_languages</code></td>
<td>possible values include: java, kotlin, objc, swift, ruby, groovy, javascript, c - explicitly prevents any other language from scanning besides the one you set here</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>only_permit_file_extensions</code></td>
<td>explicitly permit the following file extensions you set here, e.g. <code>.java</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>only_permit_files_matching_absolute_path</code></td>
<td>only the following list of absolute file paths is permitted for the file scan, e.g. <code>[/Users/user1/source/file1.java]</code>. The files should follow <code>source_directory</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>ignore_dependencies_containing</code></td>
<td>ignore every dependency included in this list of substrings, e.g. <code>java.util</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>ignore_dependencies_matching</code></td>
<td>ignore every dependency matching any of the regular expressions in this list of substrings, e.g. <code>^java\.util\.</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>ignore_entities_containing</code></td>
<td>ignore every entity included in this list of substrings, e.g. <code>NotRelevantClass</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>ignore_entities_matching</code></td>
<td>ignore every entity matching any of the regular expressions in this list of substrings, e.g. <code>^Test</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>import_aliases</code></td>
<td>define a list of import aliases, i.e. replace substrings within a full dependency path, e.g. <code>"@foo": src/foo</code> will replace any <code>@foo</code> alias by <code>src/foo</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>override_resolve_dependencies</code></td>
<td>if supported by the language parser, force every dependency in this list to be resolved</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>override_do_not_resolve_dependencies</code></td>
<td>if supported by the language parser, force every dependency in this list NOT to be resolved (i.e. treated as a global dependency)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>file_scan</code></td>
<td>perform a file scan, contains the metrics that should be applied on every source file</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>entity_scan</code></td>
<td>perform an entity scan, contains the metrics that should be applied on every entity (e.g. on every class)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>export</code></td>
<td>contains any export formats that should be created as output</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>appconfig</code></td>
<td>contains any configurable app config parameters</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">file_scan metrics</h2><a id="user-content-file_scan-metrics" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: file_scan metrics" href="#file_scan-metrics"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>

<th>value/ description</th>

<tbody>

<td>create a dependency graph structure based on source files, additional metrics will be added to the graph nodes</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>source_lines_of_code</code></td>
<td>apply a source lines of code metric to every file, create an overall metric</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>number_of_methods</code></td>
<td>apply a number of methods metric to every file, create an overall metric</td>
</tr>
<tr>

<td>apply a fan in/ fan out graph metric to every file, create an overall metric</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>louvain_modularity</code></td>
<td>apply a louvain modularity metric to every file, create an overall metric</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>tfidf</code></td>
<td>apply a tfidf metric to every file and extract relevant semantic keywords</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>ws_complexity</code></td>
<td>apply a whitespace complexity metric to every file</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>git_metrics</code></td>
<td>include some git-based metrics and try to apply them to every file</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
</tbody>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">entity_scan metrics</h2><a id="user-content-entity_scan-metrics" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: entity_scan metrics" href="#entity_scan-metrics"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>key</th>
<th>value/ description</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>dependency_graph</code></td>
<td>create a dependency graph structure based on extracted entities from files, additional metrics will be added to the graph nodes</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>inheritance_graph</code></td>
<td>create an inheritance graph structure based on extracted entities from files, additional metrics will be added to the graph nodes</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>complete_graph</code></td>
<td>create a complete graph structure (union of dependency/ inheritance graph) based on extracted entities from files, additional metrics will be added to the graph nodes</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>source_lines_of_code</code></td>
<td>apply a source lines of code metric to every entity, create an overall metric</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>number_of_methods</code></td>
<td>apply a number of methods metric to every entity, create an overall metric</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>fan_in_out</code></td>
<td>apply a fan in/ fan out graph metric to every entity, create an overall metric</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>louvain_modularity</code></td>
<td>apply a louvain modularity metric to every entity, create an overall metric</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>tfidf</code></td>
<td>apply a tfidf metric to every entity and extract relevant semantic keywords</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>

<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>radius_fan_out</code></td>

</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>radius_fan_in</code></td>
<td>node radius multiplication factor for the fan-in metric, default: <code>0.1</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>radius_louvain</code></td>

<tr>
<td><code>heatmap_score_base</code></td>

<tr>

</tbody>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Supported scan types and file extensions</h2><a id="user-content-supported-scan-types-and-file-extensions" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Supported scan types and file extensions" href="#supported-scan-types-and-file-extensions"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Emerge supports the following file extensions and scan types per language, whereas a <code>file_scan</code> simply calculates metrics and maps nodes within graph structures to scanned files and an <code>entity_scan</code> tries to extract more fine-grained entities from files e.g. classes or structs.</p>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>File extension</th>
<th>Language parser</th>
<th>Files</th>
<th>Entities</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>.java</code></td>
<td>Java</td>
<td>✅</td>
<td>✅</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>.swift</code></td>
<td>Swift</td>
<td>✅</td>
<td>✅</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>.c</code> / <code>.h</code> / <code>.hpp</code></td>
<td>C</td>
<td>✅</td>
<td>❌</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>.cpp</code> / <code>.h</code> / <code>.hpp</code></td>
<td>C++</td>
<td>✅</td>
<td>❌</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>.groovy</code></td>
<td>Groovy</td>
<td>✅</td>
<td>✅</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>.js</code> / <code>.jsx</code></td>
<td>JavaScript</td>
<td>✅</td>
<td>❌</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>.ts</code> / <code>.tsx</code></td>
<td>TypeScript</td>
<td>✅</td>
<td>❌</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>.k</code></td>
<td>Kotlin</td>
<td>✅</td>
<td>✅</td>
</tr>
<tr>
