<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Interpretation of graphs</h2><a id="user-content-interpretation-of-graphs" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Interpretation of graphs" href="#interpretation-of-graphs"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The interpretation of such graphs can often be very subjective and project dependent. The following examples should help to recognize certain patterns through indicators and hints.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">⭐️ Modularity</h3><a id="user-content-️-modularity" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: ⭐️ Modularity" href="#️-modularity"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The magic of uncovering modularity lies in applying a community detection algorithm e.g. Louvain optimization to a force-directed graph, so that both distances and coloring influence the result. The following example includes several indicators for a modular codebase.</p>
<ol dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">In the first example on the left you can spot multiple coherent colored clusters which show a low coupling by a certain distance (= generated by the force-directed graph).</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">In the second example on the right the same graph is rendered with activated cluster hulls. On this example the hulls show minimal to no overlapping. Such hints can be indicators for a good software architecture e.g. in terms of <em>modularity</em>, <em>abstraction</em> and well defined <em>interfaces</em>.</p>
</li>
</ol>
<p align="center" dir="auto">
<a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/glato/assets/emerge/modular_codebase_01.png"><img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/glato/assets/emerge/modular_codebase_01.png" width="40%" style="max-width: 100%;"></a>   <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/glato/assets/emerge/modular_codebase_02.png"><img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/glato/assets/emerge/modular_codebase_02.png" width="40%" style="max-width: 100%;"></a>
</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">⭐️ Codebases with different characteristics</h3><a id="user-content-️-codebases-with-different-characteristics" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: ⭐️ Codebases with different characteristics" href="#️-codebases-with-different-characteristics"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ol dir="auto">
<li>In the following example on the left you can see structures with increased modularity e.g. cluster 1 and 2.</li>
<li>At the same time you can see another cluster 3 that shows increased overlappings with other clusters. In addition to that an active SLOC metric even shows some huge entities (e.g. classes) which could indicate code smells like <em>god classes</em>. Such code smells like increased coupling and god classes may indicate increased maintenace efforts and error-proneness.</li>
</ol>
<p align="center" dir="auto">
<a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/glato/assets/emerge/large_codebase_01.png"><img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/glato/assets/emerge/large_codebase_01.png" width="40%" style="max-width: 100%;"></a>   <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/glato/assets/emerge/large_codebase_02.png"><img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/glato/assets/emerge/large_codebase_02.png" width="40%" style="max-width: 100%;"></a>
</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">⭐️ Big Ball of Mud</h3><a id="user-content-️-big-ball-of-mud" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: ⭐️ Big Ball of Mud" href="#️-big-ball-of-mud"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">"A BIG BALL OF MUD is haphazardly structured, sprawling, sloppy, duct-tape and bailing wire, spaghetti code jungle" (B. Foote, J. Yoder, 1997). This kind of graph often represents a less optimal architecture. To verify this kind of <em>spaghetti code jungle</em>, one can simply enable hull rendering for all clusters to finally determine: there is only one big cluster after all.</p>
<p align="center" dir="auto">
<a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/glato/assets/emerge/ball_of_mud_01.png"><img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/glato/assets/emerge/ball_of_mud_01.png" width="40%" style="max-width: 100%;"></a>   <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/glato/assets/emerge/ball_of_mud_02.png"><img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/glato/assets/emerge/ball_of_mud_02.png" width="40%" style="max-width: 100%;"></a>
</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">⭐️ Abstract irrelevant dependencies</h3><a id="user-content-️-abstract-irrelevant-dependencies" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: ⭐️ Abstract irrelevant dependencies" href="#️-abstract-irrelevant-dependencies"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Sometimes it can help to better understand the complexity of a software architecture if irrelevant dependencies are ignored.</p>
<ol dir="auto">
<li>Besides being shocked to see the Big Ball of Mud with irrelevant dependencies like java.lang, java.util or any third party dependencies that does not directly belong to a project ...</li>
<li>... you can remove irrelevant dependencies configuratively with the key <code>ignore_dependencies_containing</code> (or <code>ignore_dependencies_matching</code> if you prefer regular expressions). With a comparatively activated fan-out metric, one recognizes more scattering, some distant hub nodes and clearer clusters. All of these are possible clues to the real (= often more understandable) architecture underneath.</li>
</ol>
<p align="center" dir="auto">
<a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/glato/assets/emerge/ball_of_mud_all_dependencies.png"><img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/glato/assets/emerge/ball_of_mud_all_dependencies.png" width="43%" style="max-width: 100%;"></a>   <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/glato/assets/emerge/cleaner_graph_hub_nodes.png"><img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/glato/assets/emerge/cleaner_graph_hub_nodes.png" width="36%" style="max-width: 100%;"></a>
</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Further development</h2><a id="user-content-further-development" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Further development" href="#further-development"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Parsing of further entity types for more languages is planned for further development. <a href="/glato/emerge/blob/dev/CONTRIBUTING.md">Contributions</a> are very welcome ❤️</li>
<li>Everyone is invited to contribute to this project, whether the contribution is related with development, testing, bug reporting or any other support. I would appreciate any help. See <a href="/glato/emerge/blob/dev/CONTRIBUTING.md">Contributing</a> and <a href="/glato/emerge/blob/dev/CREDITS.md">Credits</a> for further details.</li>
</ul>
</article></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><div class="prc-PageLayout-PaneWrapper-pHPop pr-2" style="--offset-header:0px;--spacing-row:var(--spacing-none);--spacing-column:var(--spacing-none)" data-is-hidden="false" data-position="end"><div class="prc-PageLayout-HorizontalDivider-JLVqp prc-PageLayout-PaneHorizontalDivider-9tbnE" data-variant-narrow="none" data-variant-regular="none" data-position="end" style="--spacing-divider:var(--spacing-none);--spacing:var(--spacing-none)"></div><div class="prc-PageLayout-Pane-AyzHK" style="--spacing:var(--spacing-normal);--pane-min-width:256px;--pane-max-width:calc(100vw - var(--pane-max-width-diff));--pane-width-size:var(--pane-width-large);--pane-width:320px"><rails-partial data-partial-name="codeViewRepoRoute.Sidebar" class="RailsPartial-module__d-contents__G5m4w">

  <h2 class="tmp-mb-3 h4">About</h2>

      <p class="f4 tmp-my-3">
        Emerge is a browser-based interactive codebase and dependency visualization tool for many different programming languages. It supports some basic code quality and graph metrics and provides a simple and intuitive way to explore and analyze a codebase by using graph structures.
      </p>

metadata.description: Emerge is a browser-based interactive codebase and dependency visualization tool for many different programming languages. It supports some basic code quality and graph metrics and provides a simple and intuitive way to explore and analyze a codebase by using graph structures. - glato/emerge
